_meta.description: Retention cohort analysis configuration for FFE Social Engine. Defines weekly/monthly retention targets, D1/D7/D30 activation retention, and the PostHog retention insight setup instructions.
_meta.implementation_note: PostHog retention insights are configured in the PostHog UI (Insights > Retention). Use the queries below as reference for manual setup or API provisioning.

retention_analyses.name: Weekly Content Creation Retention
retention_analyses.description: Users who return to create content in week N+1 after their signup week. Measures whether users form a content creation habit.
retention_analyses.interpretation: Retained = user created at least one piece of content in week N+1 after they first signed up. Strong signal of product-market fit when week 4 > 25%.

retention_analyses.name: Monthly Post Retention
retention_analyses.description: Users who successfully post content in month N+1 after their first post. Measures long-term engagement with the publish workflow.
retention_analyses.interpretation: Retained = user published at least one post in month N+1 after first post. 6-month retention > 12% indicates sustainable usage patterns.

retention_analyses.name: D1 / D7 / D30 Activation Retention
retention_analyses.description: Classic activation retention: do users who sign up come back on day 1, day 7, and day 30?
retention_analyses.interpretation: Retained = any $pageview after signup. D1 > 40% is the industry benchmark for SaaS tools. Low D1 suggests weak activation hook or email follow-up gap.

retention_analyses.name: Onboarding Completion → Content Retention
retention_analyses.description: Of users who complete onboarding, what % are still creating content 30 days later?
retention_analyses.interpretation: Users who complete onboarding should have much higher retention than overall. If onboarding-completed retention is close to overall retention, the wizard isn't adding value.

retention_analyses.name: Trial Users — Content Engagement During Trial
retention_analyses.description: Do trial users engage with content creation during their 14-day trial? Leading indicator of conversion.
retention_analyses.interpretation: Users who post ≥ 3 times during their trial have 3x higher conversion. Use this to define the 'magic number' for trial activation campaigns.

step_1.title: Create Retention Insights in PostHog
step_1.description: Retention insights are not creatable via the dashboards API — they must be created via the Insights API or manually.
step_1.automated: Retention insights are created by posthog-provision.js --app social-engine (creates as standalone insights, then adds to dashboard)

step_2.title: Benchmark Baseline
step_2.description: After deploying, wait 4 weeks before interpreting retention charts. First 2 weeks are too early for meaningful weekly retention data.
step_2.action: Set a Linear reminder for 4 weeks post-launch to review baseline metrics

step_3.title: Export Retention Data
step_3.description: For stakeholder reports, export retention CSVs from PostHog and import to a spreadsheet. PostHog API endpoint: GET /api/projects/:id/insights/:insight_id/
step_3.schedule: Monthly — first Monday of each month
